And we were a small team, so I did all sorts of stuff from marketing to newsletters to product pages to landing pages.

That was very early on.

So that's when I got my hands deep in all of that.
We also only focused on organic traffic, so there's very little paid media that was done for all those websites.

So, yeah, so some of the examples I can give you, one of them was, like, there was a new law that came out in the U.S., a bylaw.
And there's a huge law document that was, like, 40 pages long, and I'm not a lawyer.
lawyer.
I'm not

But we saw the paper come out that morning, and then we, what I did was like, how can, and that paper was going to affect kind of the product of several of the websites that we have, or how our product can be used, it was like VPN services, so the law was going to affect that.

And so I was like, I think I can use this AI model to like summarize this document in the context of our product, of like how our product can be used, and we can write a really cool article about that, and I'll try to check everything.

So basically, I got it to write this article, I got it to write an outline first, and then I wrote, and got it to write the article, and then I fact checked it as best as I could, by essentially asking it to cite all of its claims, and then checking where those claims came from, and then anytime it made any kind of claim that I wasn't sure about, I would just kind of change the wording a little bit to not be so affirmative, just be a bit more like, this may affect this, or this may, you know.
And we posted it because we, you know, that was like that morning, the article came out, and then like the law, the law came out.
And then at the end of the day, that article was published, like I got it done in one day.
And so our that page that we posted was ranking first for like the first two weeks on Google for like, keywords very specific to it.

But of course, two weeks later, you know, you've got high authority pages like forums and all sorts of, you know, compared to the pages that we were managing, which were not super high in authority, but there were, I don't know if you know anything about it, but there were like, you know, 65 authority in certain spaces, for example, where you got like 90 plus authority pages that came in, and then they took over and then our page was no longer relevant.
But it was a cool proof of concept to see that, like, we can do this, we can get it out, and get, you know, a bunch of people to our page in that two week period.
There's other things that we like figured out, like we did a lot of product reviews.

Reviewing the same product for multiple websites, but the audience for the different websites were slightly different, or the kind of the tone, like this is an Australian site versus this is a Canadian or an American site.

So we would usually get a writer to write to review the product and then write the same review with like slight tweaks for the other pages, but they'd have to be different enough that it didn't look like, you know, and that was like just a headache for everybody.
Like the writer didn't like doing it for us for like paying a writer to do like the same work.
And so obviously, then I was like, well, we can use AI to do that.
We'll get them to write like the full, they'll do the proper review, get all the like what their opinion is on everything.
